workingTreeContext: The skill, agent-status branch label, and dev/skill audit scripts were tightened before rerunning the Codex self-dogfood flows. This evidence bundle, review result, regenerated claim artifacts, transcript artifacts, and matching implementation changes are intended to be committed together.
summary: Codex dev/skill dogfood now proves the first-scan branch states entries and linked Markdown depth before discovery, asks for scan-scope confirmation or adjustment, keeps LLM claim review behind a separate review budget, requires user confirmation of that budget before reviewer launch, and continues from raw discovery into claim review and eval planning instead of treating the raw packet as the finished answer.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: Cautilus bundled skill branch behavior under the Codex dev/skill runtime
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: This bundle proves maintained dev/skill fixture behavior for the Cautilus bundled skill and checked-in audit scripts. It does not prove arbitrary consumer agents, arbitrary host adapters, app/product runners, or every future model backend.
notClaimed: Every model provider will phrase the branch choices identically
Every consumer adapter has an honest app runner
The raw claim packet itself proves the discovered claims
Reviewer result application proves fixture authoring or eval execution
Claude dogfood was rerun in this slice

protectedBehaviors: agent status exposes a first-scan branch whose label and reason require scope confirmation before discovery
the transcript states entry files and linked Markdown depth before claim discover
the transcript asks the coordinator to confirm or adjust the scan scope before discovery
the transcript keeps LLM review as a later branch with a review budget
the flow runs first discovery, claim show, and deterministic review-input preparation before reviewer launch
the transcript states maximum clusters, claims per cluster, parallel lanes, excerpt chars, retry policy, and skipped-cluster policy before reviewer launch
the transcript includes a user confirmation turn after the budget statement and before reviewer launch
the flow writes a cautilus.claim_review_result.v1 packet and stops before apply-result, eval planning, product edits, or commits
the flow states the selected review budget and receives user confirmation before reviewer launch
the flow applies the review-result packet, validates the reviewed claim packet, and only then plans evals
the flow stops before fixture authoring, eval execution, product edits, or commits
agent status keeps the first-scan command discover-only
agent status branch copy exposes scan-scope confirmation and LLM review budget boundaries
first-scan audits reject discovery that lacks pre-discovery entries/depth or scope confirmation
reviewer-launch and review-to-eval audits reject reviewer launch that omits required review budget fields before launch
reviewer-launch and review-to-eval audits reject reviewer launch without user budget confirmation after the budget statement
loose command_execution transcript shapes retain command-order evidence

claimSupport.reason: The first-scan dogfood transcript and audit prove the skill states `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and linked Markdown depth 3 before running `claim discover`.
The first-scan dogfood transcript and audit prove the user is asked to confirm the current scan scope or adjust it before discovery.
The reviewer-launch and review-to-eval dogfood transcripts and audits prove a user confirmation turn appears after the selected review budget is stated and before the reviewer lane launches.

The reviewer-launch and review-to-eval dogfood flows prove the skill moves from raw discovery into claim review and eval planning while preserving packet boundaries, rather than treating raw discovery as a finished answer.

decision.reason: The first-scan, reviewer-launch, and review-to-eval fixtures pass under Codex with audits that encode scan-scope confirmation, required review-budget fields, explicit user budget confirmation before reviewer launch, branch progression, and raw-discovery-not-final packet boundaries.
